segments.text: The proverbs of Solomon the son of David, king of Israel
To know wisdom and instruction; to perceive the words of understanding
To receive the instruction of wisdom, justice, and judgment, and equity
To give subtilty to the simple, to the young man knowledge and discretion
A wise man will hear, and will increase learning; and a man of understanding shall attain unto wise counsels
To understand a proverb, and the interpretation; the words of the wise, and their dark sayings

footnotes: the interpretation: or, an eloquent speech

segments.text: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge
but fools despise wisdom and instruction

footnotes: the beginning: or, the principal part
heading: The Fear of the LORD

segments.text: My son, hear the instruction of thy father, and forsake not the law of thy mother
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: For they shall be an ornament of grace unto thy head, and chains about thy neck
My son, if sinners entice thee, consent thou not
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: If they say, Come with us, let us lay wait for blood, let us lurk privily for the innocent without cause
segments.speaker: Sinners

segments.text: Let us swallow them up alive as the grave; and whole, as those that go down into the pit
segments.speaker: Sinners

segments.text: We shall find all precious substance, we shall fill our houses with spoil
segments.speaker: Sinners

segments.text: Cast in thy lot among us; let us all have one purse
segments.speaker: Sinners

segments.text: My son, walk not thou in the way with them; refrain thy foot from their path
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: For their feet run to evil, and make haste to shed blood
Surely in vain the net is spread in the sight of any bird

footnotes: in the sight: Heb. in the eyes of every thing that hath a wing

segments.text: And they lay wait for their own blood; they lurk privily for their own lives
So are the ways of every one that is greedy of gain; which taketh away the life of the owners thereof
Wisdom crieth without; she uttereth her voice in the streets

footnotes: Wisdom: Heb. Wisdoms, that is, Excellent wisdom
heading: Wisdom's Call

segments.text: She crieth in the chief place of concourse, in the openings of the gates: in the city she uttereth her words, saying
How long, ye simple ones, will ye love simplicity? and the scorners delight in their scorning, and fools hate knowledge?
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: Turn you at my reproof: behold, I will pour out my spirit unto you, I will make known my words unto you
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: Because I have called, and ye refused; I have stretched out my hand, and no man regarded
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: But ye have set at nought all my counsel, and would none of my reproof
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when your fear cometh
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: When your fear cometh as desolation, and your destruction cometh as a whirlwind; when distress and anguish cometh upon you
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: Then shall they call upon me, but I will not answer; they shall seek me early, but they shall not find me
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: For that they hated knowledge, and did not choose the fear of the LORD
They would none of my counsel: they despised all my reproof
segments.speaker: Wisdom

segments.text: Therefore shall they eat of the fruit of their own way, and be filled with their own devices
For the turning away of the simple shall slay them, and the prosperity of fools shall destroy them

footnotes: turning away: or, ease of the simple

segments.text: But whoso hearkeneth unto me shall dwell safely, and shall be quiet from fear of evil
